components.role: Bass control — 250kA log pot; adjusts the low-frequency shelf level
Mid control — 25kA log pot (or 10k); adjusts mid-frequency dip depth
Treble control — 250kA log pot; adjusts high-frequency level
Slope resistor — sets the interaction between bass and mid controls
Treble cap — sets treble turnover frequency (~250pF typical)
Bass cap — sets bass turnover frequency (~22nF typical)
Mid cap — sets the mid-scoop center frequency (~22nF typical)

dsp_model.description: Complex passive 3-band EQ approximated as parametric 3-band filter; inherently lossy with ~12dB insertion loss

behavioral_description: The iconic Fender tonestack used in Deluxe, Twin Reverb, and other classic amps. This is a purely passive circuit — no active components — which means it can only cut, never boost. The characteristic 'scooped' mid dip is inherent to the topology and present at all control settings. There is always significant insertion loss (typically 10-15dB), which must be compensated by surrounding gain stages. The three controls are highly interactive: adjusting bass affects mid and treble response, and vice versa. The treble control has the most dramatic effect, while the mid pot primarily controls the depth of the mid-scoop. This interactive, lossy behavior is actually part of the Fender magic — the complex frequency shaping creates the shimmering, bell-like clean tones associated with Fender amps. Many pedals replicate this exact topology for authentic Fender-voiced tone shaping.
